# Ralphify — Full Documentation

> A minimal CLI harness for autonomous AI coding loops. Run commands, assemble a prompt, pipe it to an agent, and repeat with fresh context each iteration.

Source: https://github.com/computerlovetech/ralphify
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/ralphify/
Docs: https://ralphify.co/docs/

---

# Home

Ralphify is a minimal CLI harness for autonomous AI coding loops, inspired by the Ralph Wiggum technique (https://ghuntley.com/ralph/). The core idea fits in one line:

```bash
while :; do cat RALPH.md | claude -p ; done
```

Ralphify wraps this into a proper tool — running commands that feed test results and context into each iteration, tracking progress, and handling clean shutdown.

## Install

```bash
uv tool install ralphify
```

Also available via `pipx install ralphify` or `pip install ralphify`.

## Create a ralph and run it

```bash
ralph init my-ralph
```

This creates a directory with a `RALPH.md` template. Edit it to fit your project:

**`my-ralph/RALPH.md`**

```markdown
---
agent: claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions
commands:
  - name: tests
    run: uv run pytest -x
  - name: git-log
    run: git log --oneline -10
---

# Prompt

## Recent commits

{{ commands.git-log }}

## Test results

{{ commands.tests }}

You are an autonomous coding agent running in a loop. Each iteration
starts with a fresh context. Your progress lives in the code and git.

Read TODO.md for the current task list. Pick the top uncompleted task,
implement it fully, then mark it done.

If tests are failing, fix them before starting new work.

## Rules

- One task per iteration
- No placeholder code — full, working implementations only
- Commit with a descriptive message like `feat: add X` or `fix: resolve Y`
- Mark the completed task in TODO.md
```

```bash
ralph run my-ralph         # Start the loop (Ctrl+C to stop)
ralph run my-ralph -n 3    # Run 3 iterations
```

Edit `RALPH.md` while the loop is running — changes take effect on the next iteration.

## Or grab one from GitHub

```bash
ralph add owner/repo/my-ralph    # Install a ralph from GitHub
ralph run my-ralph               # Run it
```

## Why ralph loops?

A single agent conversation fills up its context window, slows down, and eventually loses the plot. Ralph loops solve this by resetting every iteration — the agent always starts fresh.

- **Fresh context, no decay** — each iteration starts with a clean context window. No conversation bloat, no hallucinated memories.
- **Commands as feedback** — commands run each iteration and their output feeds into the prompt. When tests fail, the agent sees the failure and fixes it.
- **Steer while it runs** — the prompt is re-read every iteration. Edit RALPH.md while the loop runs.
- **Progress lives in git** — every iteration commits to git. Roll back with `git reset` if needed.

## Requirements

- Python 3.11+
- Claude Code CLI (or any agent CLI that accepts piped input)

---

# When to Use Ralph Loops

Ralph loops are powerful but they're not the right tool for everything. This page helps you decide whether a loop fits your task.

## The sweet spot

Ralph loops work best when a task has these properties:

**Decomposable into small, independent steps.** The loop does one thing per iteration. Tasks that naturally break into "do this, then this, then this" are ideal.

**Has a clear definition of "done" for each step.** Commands need something to validate. If you can express "this iteration succeeded" as a command that exits 0 or non-zero, the self-healing loop works.

**Benefits from fresh context.** Long conversations degrade. If your task will take more than 15-20 minutes of agent work, a loop outperforms a single conversation.

**Progress is visible in the codebase.** The agent's work must be observable on disk — files changed, tests added, docs written, commits made.

## What works well

- Implementing features from a spec — each feature is one iteration; test commands validate correctness
- Writing tests — each module is one iteration; coverage commands guide prioritization
- Fixing lint / type errors — each fix is small and independently verifiable
- Documentation improvements — each page is one iteration; build commands validate
- Codebase migrations (JS to TS, Python 2 to 3) — each file is one iteration
- Bug triage — each bug is one iteration; regression tests verify the fix
- Refactoring — each extraction/rename is one iteration; tests catch regressions
- Security hardening — run a scanner each iteration; the agent picks one finding and fixes it
- Research & knowledge building — each iteration deepens one area; a report file accumulates findings

## What doesn't work well

- Design decisions — requires human judgment about trade-offs
- Tasks requiring multi-step reasoning across iterations — each iteration starts fresh
- One-shot tasks — if the task takes 5 minutes, just chat with the agent
- Tasks with no automated validation — without command feedback, there's no self-healing
- Creative writing — prose quality is subjective
- Interacting with external services — API calls and deployments are hard to undo

## Loop vs. single conversation

Use a **single conversation** when the task takes less than 10-15 minutes, requires interactive iteration, back-and-forth discussion, or subjective decisions.

Use a **ralph loop** when the task involves many similar independent steps, you want autonomous operation, you have validation commands, or the task would fill up a conversation's context window.

## How many iterations?

- Start with `-n 3` to verify your setup
- Use `-n 10-20` for bounded tasks
- Run unlimited for open-ended improvement tasks with good command feedback
- Use `--stop-on-error` when each iteration must succeed before the next
- Set `--timeout` as a safety net for unattended runs

---

# Getting Started

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.11+
- An AI coding agent CLI — this tutorial uses Claude Code. Install with `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`.
- A project with a test suite

## Step 1: Install ralphify

```bash
uv tool install ralphify
ralph --version
```

## Step 2: Create a ralph

```bash
ralph init my-ralph
```

This creates `my-ralph/RALPH.md` with a ready-to-customize template. Alternatively, use `ralph new` for AI-guided setup, or create the file manually.

### Manual setup

Create a ralph directory and `RALPH.md` with the agent field — this is the only required frontmatter:

```markdown
---
agent: claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions
---

You are an autonomous coding agent running in a loop. Each iteration
starts with a fresh context. Your progress lives in the code and git.

Read TODO.md for the current task list. Pick the top uncompleted task,
implement it fully, then mark it done.

## Rules

- One task per iteration
- No placeholder code — full, working implementations only
- Run `uv run pytest -x` before committing
- Commit with a descriptive message like `feat: add X` or `fix: resolve Y`
- Mark the completed task in TODO.md
```

The `-p` flag enables non-interactive mode (reads prompt from stdin). The `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag disables approval prompts so the agent can work autonomously.

## Step 3: Do a test run

```bash
ralph run my-ralph -n 1 --log-dir ralph_logs
```

Review the log to see what the agent did:

```bash
ls ralph_logs/
cat ralph_logs/001_*.log
```

## Step 4: Add a test command

Commands run each iteration and their output is injected into the prompt via `{{ commands.<name> }}` placeholders:

```markdown
---
agent: claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions
commands:
  - name: tests
    run: uv run pytest -x
---

## Test results

{{ commands.tests }}

You are an autonomous coding agent running in a loop. Each iteration
starts with a fresh context. Your progress lives in the code and git.

If tests are failing, fix them before starting new work.
```

## Step 5: Add more commands

```yaml
commands:
  - name: tests
    run: uv run pytest -x
  - name: lint
    run: uv run ruff check .
  - name: git-log
    run: git log --oneline -10
```

## Step 6: Run the loop

```bash
ralph run my-ralph -n 3 --log-dir ralph_logs
```

Once confident, drop `-n 3` to run indefinitely. Press Ctrl+C to stop.

## Step 7: Steer while it runs

The prompt body is re-read every iteration. Edit RALPH.md while the loop runs — changes take effect on the next cycle.

Note: Frontmatter fields (agent, commands, args) are parsed once at startup. If you change those, restart the loop.

---

# How it Works

## The iteration lifecycle

Every iteration follows the same sequence:

1. Re-read RALPH.md from disk
2. Run commands
3. Resolve placeholders
4. Assemble prompt
5. Pipe prompt to agent
6. Wait for agent to exit
7. Repeat

### 1. Re-read RALPH.md

The prompt body is read from disk every iteration. Edit the prompt text while the loop is running — changes take effect on the next cycle. Frontmatter fields are parsed once at startup.

### 2. Run commands

Each command runs in order and captures stdout + stderr. Commands run from the project root by default. Commands starting with `./` run relative to the ralph directory.

Command output is captured regardless of exit code. Each command has a default timeout of 60 seconds, configurable with the `timeout` field.

### 3. Resolve placeholders

- `{{ commands.<name> }}` — replaced with the corresponding command's output
- `{{ args.<name> }}` — replaced with user argument values from the CLI
- `{{ ralph.name }}` — ralph directory name
- `{{ ralph.iteration }}` — current iteration number (1-based)
- `{{ ralph.max_iterations }}` — total iterations if `-n` was set, empty otherwise

Only commands referenced by a placeholder appear in the prompt. Unmatched placeholders resolve to empty string.

### 4. Assemble the prompt

The prompt body with all placeholders resolved becomes the fully assembled prompt. HTML comments (`<!-- ... -->`) are stripped during assembly.

By default, ralphify appends a co-author trailer instruction asking the agent to include `Co-authored-by: Ralphify <noreply@ralphify.co>` in commits. Disable with `credit: false` in frontmatter.

### 5. Pipe prompt to agent

```bash
echo "<assembled prompt>" | claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions
```

When the agent command starts with `claude`, ralphify automatically adds `--output-format stream-json --verbose` for structured streaming.

### 6. Repeat

The loop starts the next iteration from step 1 with fresh command output and the current state of the codebase.

## What gets re-read each iteration

- Prompt body: every iteration (edit while running)
- Command output: every iteration (fresh data)
- Frontmatter (agent, commands, args): once at startup (restart to change)
- User arguments: once at startup (constant for the run)

## Stop conditions

- Ctrl+C (first): gracefully finishes current iteration, then stops
- Ctrl+C (second): force-stops immediately
- `-n` limit reached: stops after specified iterations
- `--stop-on-error` and agent exits non-zero or times out: stops after current iteration
- `--timeout` exceeded: agent killed, iteration marked as timed out, loop continues (unless --stop-on-error)

---

# Writing Effective Loop Prompts

Your RALPH.md is the single most important file in a ralph loop. It defines the agent, the commands, and the prompt.

## The anatomy of a good RALPH.md

### 1. Role and orientation

Tell the agent what it is and how it works:

```markdown
You are an autonomous coding agent running in a loop. Each iteration
starts with a fresh context. Your progress lives in the code and git.
```

### 2. Task source

Point the agent at something concrete:

- `Read TODO.md and pick the top uncompleted task` — when you maintain a task list
- `Read PLAN.md and implement the next step` — for sequential multi-step work
- `Find the module with the lowest test coverage` — for coverage-driven testing
- `Fix the failing tests shown above` — when commands feed failures back

### 3. Rules and constraints

Constraints are more important than instructions:

```markdown
## Rules

- One task per iteration
- No placeholder code — full, working implementations only
- Run tests before committing
- Commit with a descriptive message
- Do not modify existing tests to make them pass
```

## Shell features in commands

Commands are run directly, not through a shell — pipes, redirections, and chaining don't work. Use a script as an escape hatch:

```yaml
commands:
  - name: status
    run: ./check-status.sh
```

Commands starting with `./` run relative to the ralph directory.

## Patterns that work

### The TODO-driven loop

Agent reads TODO.md, picks one task, implements it, marks it done. You control priority by reordering the list.

### The self-healing loop

Rely on command output to define "done" and let failures guide the agent.

### The edit-while-running loop

Edit RALPH.md while the loop runs to steer the agent in real time. Add constraints when the agent does something wrong, change the task when you want to shift focus.

### Parameterized ralphs

Use `args` to make a ralph reusable:

```markdown
---
agent: claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions
args: [dir, focus]
commands:
  - name: git-log
    run: git log --oneline -10 -- {{ args.dir }}
---

Research the codebase at {{ args.dir }}.
Focus area: {{ args.focus }}
```

```bash
ralph run research --dir ./api --focus "error handling"
```

### Ralph context placeholders

- `{{ ralph.name }}` — ralph directory name
- `{{ ralph.iteration }}` — current iteration number (1-based)
- `{{ ralph.max_iterations }}` — total iterations if `-n` was set

Use these for iteration-aware prompts — telling the agent to wrap up on the last iteration, or including the ralph name in commit messages.

## Anti-patterns to avoid

- **Too vague** — "Improve the codebase" gives no direction
- **Too many tasks per iteration** — one iteration = one task
- **No validation step** — without tests or a command, the agent will commit broken code
- **No commit instructions** — ralphify doesn't commit for the agent; explicit commit instructions are needed

## Tuning a running loop

- When the agent does something dumb, add a sign (a rule)
- When the agent gets stuck in a loop, add explicit fallback instructions
- When you want to shift focus, edit the task source
- When the agent is too ambitious, tighten the scope constraint

HTML comments in RALPH.md are stripped before prompt assembly — use them for notes to yourself.

## Prompt size

- Core prompt: 20-50 lines is the sweet spot
- Commands: pick the 2-3 most useful signals
- Use scripts to filter verbose command output

---

# Using with Different Agents

Ralphify works with any CLI that reads a prompt from stdin and exits when done.

## What ralphify needs from an agent

1. Read a prompt from stdin
2. Do work in the current directory
3. Exit when done (exit code 0 = success, non-zero = failure)

## Claude Code (recommended)

```markdown
---
agent: claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions
---
```

Install: `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`

Ralphify auto-adds `--output-format stream-json --verbose` for real-time activity tracking.

## Aider

```markdown
---
agent: bash -c 'aider --yes-always --no-auto-commits --message "$(cat -)"'
---
```

Aider doesn't natively read from stdin, so the bash wrapper reads stdin with `cat -` and passes it as a `--message` argument.

## Codex CLI

```markdown
---
agent: codex exec --sandbox danger-full-access -
---
```

## Custom wrapper script

For full control, write a wrapper script that reads stdin and calls your agent:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e
PROMPT=$(cat -)
my-custom-agent --input "$PROMPT" --auto-approve
```

```markdown
---
agent: ./ralph-agent.sh
---
```

## Testing your setup

```bash
echo "Say hello" | <your-agent-command>
ralph run my-ralph -n 1 --log-dir ralph_logs
```

---

# Cookbook

Copy-pasteable setups for common autonomous workflows. All recipes use Claude Code as the agent.

## Run autonomous ML experiments

An autonomous ML research loop inspired by Karpathy's autoresearch. The agent runs experiments on a training script to minimize validation loss.

```bash
ralph run autoresearch --train_script train.py --prepare_script prepare.py
```

## Improve code quality continuously

A loop that runs tests, type checking, and lint each iteration, then picks one improvement.

```bash
ralph run improve-codebase -n 10 --log-dir ralph_logs
ralph run improve-codebase -n 5 --focus "focus on test coverage"
```

## Write and improve documentation automatically

```bash
ralph run docs --log-dir ralph_logs
ralph run docs --focus "focus on the API reference pages"
```

## Find and fix bugs automatically

The agent reads the codebase, finds a real bug, writes a failing test, then fixes it.

```bash
ralph run bug-hunter -n 10 --log-dir ralph_logs
```

## Run structured AI research loops

A structured research loop with helper scripts, args for the research topic, and a timeout on the review command.

```bash
ralph run research --workspace ai-safety --focus "current approaches to AI alignment"
```

## Migrate code patterns across a codebase

A loop for batch code transformations with a `remaining` command that counts files still needing migration.

```bash
ralph run migrate --old_pattern "from utils import legacy_helper" \
                  --new_pattern "from core.helpers import modern_helper"
```

## Automate security scanning and fixes

The agent runs a scanner each iteration, picks one finding, fixes it, and verifies the fix.

```bash
ralph run security -n 10 --log-dir ralph_logs
```

## Increase test coverage automatically

The agent sees current coverage and uncovered functions, then writes tests for one module per iteration.

```bash
ralph run test-coverage -n 10 --log-dir ralph_logs
```

---

# Quick Reference

## CLI commands

```bash
ralph run my-ralph                 # Run loop forever (Ctrl+C to stop)
ralph run my-ralph/RALPH.md        # Can also pass the file path directly
ralph run my-ralph -n 5            # Run 5 iterations
ralph run my-ralph -n 1 --log-dir logs  # Single iteration with output capture
ralph run my-ralph --stop-on-error # Stop if agent exits non-zero or times out
ralph run my-ralph --delay 10      # Wait 10s between iterations
ralph run my-ralph --timeout 300   # Kill agent after 5 min per iteration
ralph run my-ralph --dir ./src     # Pass user args to the ralph

ralph init my-task                  # Scaffold a ralph from template (no AI)
ralph init                         # Scaffold in current directory

ralph new                          # AI-guided ralph creation
ralph new docs                     # AI-guided creation with name pre-filled

ralph add owner/repo               # Install ralph(s) from a GitHub repo
ralph add owner/repo/my-ralph      # Install a specific ralph by name

ralph --version                    # Show version
```

## Directory structure

```text
my-ralph/
└── RALPH.md              # Prompt + configuration (required)
```

A ralph is a directory with a RALPH.md file. That's it.

## RALPH.md format

```markdown
---
agent: claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions    # Required: agent command
commands:                                           # Optional: run each iteration
  - name: tests
    run: uv run pytest -x
  - name: lint
    run: uv run ruff check .
args: [dir, focus]                                  # Optional: declared user arguments
---

# Prompt body

{{ commands.tests }}

{{ commands.lint }}

Your instructions here. Use {{ args.dir }} for user arguments.
```

### Frontmatter fields

- `agent` (string, required) — full agent command
- `commands` (list, optional) — commands to run each iteration
- `args` (list, optional) — user argument names
- `credit` (bool, optional) — co-author trailer instruction (default: true)

### Command fields

- `name` (string, required) — identifier for `{{ commands.<name> }}`
- `run` (string, required) — shell command to execute
- `timeout` (number, default 60) — max seconds before killed

## Placeholders

- `{{ commands.<name> }}` — command output (stdout + stderr)
- `{{ args.<name> }}` — user argument values from CLI
- `{{ ralph.name }}` — ralph directory name
- `{{ ralph.iteration }}` — current iteration number (1-based)
- `{{ ralph.max_iterations }}` — total iterations if `-n` was set

Unmatched placeholders resolve to empty string.

## The loop

1. Re-read RALPH.md from disk
2. Run all commands in order, capture output
3. Resolve placeholders
4. Pipe assembled prompt to agent via stdin
5. Wait for agent to exit
6. Repeat

## Stopping the loop

- Ctrl+C (once): finishes current iteration gracefully, then stops
- Ctrl+C (twice): force-kills agent and exits immediately
- `-n` limit reached: stops after specified iterations
- `--stop-on-error`: stops if agent exits non-zero or times out

## `ralph add` source formats

- `owner/repo` — all ralphs in the repo
- `owner/repo/my-ralph` — specific ralph by name
- `https://github.com/owner/repo` — full URL
- `https://github.com/owner/repo/tree/main/my-ralph` — URL from GitHub browser

Installed ralphs go to `.ralphify/ralphs/<name>/`.

---

# CLI Reference

## `ralph`

```bash
ralph            # Show banner and help
ralph --version  # Show version
ralph --help     # Show help
```

## `ralph run`

Start the autonomous coding loop.

```bash
ralph run my-ralph                         # Run forever (Ctrl+C to stop)
ralph run my-ralph -n 5                    # Run 5 iterations
ralph run my-ralph --stop-on-error         # Stop if agent exits non-zero or times out
ralph run my-ralph --delay 10              # Wait 10s between iterations
ralph run my-ralph --timeout 300           # Kill agent after 5 minutes per iteration
ralph run my-ralph --log-dir ralph_logs    # Save output to log files
```

Options:
- `PATH` (required) — path to ralph directory, RALPH.md file, or installed ralph name
- `-n` — max iterations (default: unlimited)
- `--stop-on-error` / `-s` — stop on non-zero exit or timeout
- `--delay` / `-d` — seconds between iterations (default: 0)
- `--timeout` / `-t` — max seconds per iteration
- `--log-dir` / `-l` — directory for log files

### User arguments

Pass named flags after the ralph path: `ralph run research --dir ./src --focus "perf"`

Named flags work without frontmatter declaration. The `args` field is only required for positional arguments.

```bash
# Named flags
ralph run research --dir ./my-project --focus "performance"

# Positional args (requires args: [dir, focus] in frontmatter)
ralph run research ./my-project "performance"

# -- ends flag parsing
ralph run research -- --verbose ./src
```

### Stopping the loop

- Ctrl+C (first): finishes current iteration gracefully
- Ctrl+C (second): force-kills agent process

## `ralph init`

Scaffold a new ralph with a template.

```bash
ralph init my-task      # Creates my-task/RALPH.md
ralph init              # Creates RALPH.md in current directory
```

## `ralph new`

AI-guided ralph creation.

```bash
ralph new              # Agent helps you choose name and build everything
ralph new my-task      # Start with a name already chosen
```

## `ralph add`

Install a ralph from GitHub to `.ralphify/ralphs/<name>/`.

```bash
ralph add owner/repo                                        # All ralphs in repo
ralph add owner/repo/ralph-name                             # Specific ralph
ralph add https://github.com/owner/repo                     # Full URL
ralph add https://github.com/owner/repo/tree/main/my-ralph  # URL from GitHub
```

## RALPH.md format

Frontmatter fields:
- `agent` (string, required) — agent command
- `commands` (list, optional) — commands with `name`, `run`, optional `timeout` (default 60)
- `args` (list of strings, optional) — declared argument names
- `credit` (bool, optional) — co-author trailer (default: true)

Placeholders:
- `{{ commands.<name> }}` — command output
- `{{ args.<name> }}` — user argument value
- `{{ ralph.name }}` — ralph directory name
- `{{ ralph.iteration }}` — iteration number (1-based)
- `{{ ralph.max_iterations }}` — total iterations if `-n` set

---

# Python API

Use ralphify as a Python library to embed the loop in automation pipelines.

```python
from ralphify import run_loop, RunConfig, RunState
```

## Quick start

```python
from pathlib import Path
from ralphify import run_loop, RunConfig, RunState

config = RunConfig(
    agent="claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions",
    ralph_dir=Path("my-ralph"),
    ralph_file=Path("my-ralph/RALPH.md"),
    commands=[],
    max_iterations=3,
)
state = RunState(run_id="my-run")
run_loop(config, state)
```

## RunConfig

All settings for a single run:

- `agent` (str) — agent command string
- `ralph_dir` (Path) — ralph directory path
- `ralph_file` (Path) — RALPH.md file path
- `commands` (list[Command]) — commands to run each iteration
- `args` (dict[str, str]) — user argument values
- `max_iterations` (int | None) — max iterations (None = unlimited)
- `delay` (float) — seconds between iterations
- `timeout` (float | None) — max seconds per iteration
- `stop_on_error` (bool) — stop on non-zero exit
- `log_dir` (Path | None) — log file directory
- `credit` (bool) — co-author trailer (default: True)
- `project_root` (Path) — project root directory

## Command

```python
from ralphify import Command
cmd = Command(name="tests", run="uv run pytest -x")
cmd_slow = Command(name="integration", run="uv run pytest tests/integration", timeout=300)
```

## RunState

Observable state for a running loop with thread-safe control:

```python
state = RunState(run_id="my-run")
run_loop(config, state)

print(state.status)       # RunStatus.COMPLETED
print(state.completed)    # 4
print(state.failed)       # 1
print(state.total)        # 5

state.request_stop()      # Stop after current iteration
state.request_pause()     # Pause between iterations
state.request_resume()    # Resume a paused loop
```

## RunStatus

PENDING, RUNNING, PAUSED, STOPPED, COMPLETED, FAILED

## Event system

The loop emits structured events. Implement the EventEmitter protocol (single `emit(event)` method).

### Event types

Run lifecycle: RUN_STARTED, RUN_STOPPED, RUN_PAUSED, RUN_RESUMED
Iteration lifecycle: ITERATION_STARTED, ITERATION_COMPLETED, ITERATION_FAILED, ITERATION_TIMED_OUT
Commands: COMMANDS_STARTED, COMMANDS_COMPLETED
Prompt: PROMPT_ASSEMBLED
Other: AGENT_ACTIVITY, LOG_MESSAGE

### Built-in emitters

- NullEmitter — discards events (default)
- QueueEmitter — pushes to queue.Queue
- FanoutEmitter — broadcasts to multiple emitters
- BoundEmitter — wraps emitter with fixed run_id

## Concurrent runs with RunManager

```python
from ralphify import RunManager, RunConfig, Command

manager = RunManager()
run = manager.create_run(config)
manager.start_run(run.state.run_id)

for run in manager.list_runs():
    print(f"{run.state.run_id}: {run.state.status.value}")

manager.pause_run(run_id)
manager.resume_run(run_id)
manager.stop_run(run_id)
```

---

# Troubleshooting

## Quick checklist

1. Run `ralph run my-ralph -n 1` — validates setup and shows clear errors
2. Test agent outside ralphify: `echo "Say hello" | claude -p`
3. Use `--log-dir ralph_logs` to capture output
4. Commands don't support shell features (pipes, `&&`) — use a wrapper script

## Setup issues

### "is not a directory, RALPH.md file, or installed ralph"

Check path exists: `ls my-ralph/RALPH.md` or `ls .ralphify/ralphs/my-ralph/RALPH.md`

### "Missing or empty 'agent' field in RALPH.md frontmatter"

Add `agent:` to your frontmatter.

### "Agent command 'claude' not found on PATH"

Install the agent CLI or check your PATH.

## `ralph add` issues

### "Cannot parse source"

Use: `owner/repo`, `owner/repo/ralph-name`, or a full GitHub URL.

### "git clone failed"

Check repo exists and you have access. Private repos require git credentials.

### "No RALPH.md found"

The repo was cloned but contains no RALPH.md files.

## Loop issues

### Agent hangs

Test directly: `echo "Say hello" | claude -p`. Use `--timeout` to kill stalled iterations.

### Agent exits non-zero every iteration

Use `--log-dir` and check logs. Common: missing auth, failing commands, prompt too large.

### Agent doesn't commit

Add explicit commit instructions to the prompt. Ensure agent has git permissions.

### Loop runs too fast / no useful work

Check logs. Usually: vague prompt, no task source, or agent can't find work.

## Frontmatter issues

### Invalid characters in names

Command and arg names: letters, digits, hyphens, underscores only.

### Duplicate names

Command and arg names must be unique.

### Wrong types

`args` must be a list of strings. `commands` must be a list of {name, run} mappings.

## Command issues

### Invalid syntax

Check quoting in `run` value. Use a script for complex commands.

### Binary not found

Verify binary exists. Use `uv run` prefix for virtual environment tools.

### Pipes or redirections not working

Commands use `shlex.split()` — no shell features. Point to a script instead.

### Command timeout

Default: 60 seconds. Increase with `timeout` field per command.

### Command output missing from prompt

Check name matches exactly. Must be `commands` (plural), not `command`.

## Common questions

### Can I run multiple loops in parallel?

Yes, on separate branches. Or use RunManager from the Python API.

### Can I edit RALPH.md while the loop runs?

Yes. Prompt body is re-read every iteration. Frontmatter requires a restart.

### How do I disable co-author credit?

Set `credit: false` in frontmatter.

### How do I make a ralph reusable?

Use `args` to parameterize values: `ralph run my-ralph --dir ./api --focus "errors"`

---

# Changelog

## 0.3.0 — 2026-03-24

- `ralph add` — install ralphs from GitHub
- Two-stage Ctrl+C — graceful then force stop
- Iteration monitor UI with Rich markdown rendering
- Process group isolation for agent subprocesses

## 0.2.5 — 2026-03-22

- Ralph placeholders: `{{ ralph.name }}`, `{{ ralph.iteration }}`, `{{ ralph.max_iterations }}`

## 0.2.4 — 2026-03-22

- Fixed placeholder cross-contamination between args and commands
- Helpful errors for missing binaries and invalid syntax
- Fixed timed-out output not echoed, `--` separator, garbled output, frontmatter parsing edge cases
- Fixed arg values with spaces, Windows streaming mode detection, empty arg values

## 0.2.3 — 2026-03-21

- Co-authored-by credit trailer (opt out with `credit: false`)

## 0.2.2 — 2026-03-21

- `ralph init` command — scaffold without AI

## 0.2.1 — 2026-03-21

- Fixed `{{ args.* }}` placeholders in command `run` strings

## 0.2.0 — 2026-03-21

The v2 rewrite. A ralph is now a directory with a RALPH.md file. Removed ralph.toml, .ralphify/ directory, checks, contexts, and instructions. Everything lives in one file.

## 0.1.x

See full changelog at https://ralphify.co/docs/changelog/

---

# Contributing

## Development setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/computerlovetech/ralphify.git
cd ralphify
uv sync
uv run ralph --version
```

## Running tests

```bash
uv run pytest
uv run pytest -x
```

Tests use temporary directories with no external dependencies.

## Documentation

```bash
uv run mkdocs serve        # Local preview at http://127.0.0.1:8000
uv run mkdocs build --strict  # Build check (CI uses --strict)
```

## Submitting changes

1. Fork and branch from main
2. Make focused, atomic commits
3. Run tests: `uv run pytest`
4. Check docs if changed: `uv run mkdocs build --strict`
5. Open a PR with clear description

Commit message style: `docs: explain X`, `feat: add X so users can Y`, `fix: resolve X that caused Y`

## Dependencies

Runtime: typer, rich, pyyaml. Minimal by design — prefer stdlib.
